differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Automatic dependency tracking for precise updates without VDOM overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Uses JavaScript Proxies for tracking changes, benefits from VDOM diffing.

differences.title: 2. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Compiles JSX/templates into performant, minimal vanilla JavaScript.
differences.rightDesc: Can compile at build time or use a runtime compiler.

differences.title: 3. VIRTUAL DOM
differences.leftDesc: Directly manipulates the DOM for unparalleled performance.
differences.rightDesc: Compares VDOM trees to determine minimal DOM updates.

differences.title: 4. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Extremely small initial bundle sizes due to no runtime overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Generally larger bundles due to runtime features and compiler.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Often tops benchmarks for rendering and update performance.
differences.rightDesc: Highly performant, but typically trails SolidJS in raw speed.

differences.title: 6. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Requires understanding of signals and reactivity primitives.
differences.rightDesc: Familiar concepts for many developers, easier to grasp initially.

differences.title: 7. ECOSYSTEM & COMMUNITY
differences.leftDesc: Smaller but rapidly expanding community and toolset.
differences.rightDesc: Large, established community, extensive libraries and tooling.
